I kind of wish I had ordered a Large in this swimsuit. I ordered it so that I could wear a more modest swimsuit to the classes at my local aquatic center. It's great for that, and I'm in a class with a lot of middle-aged women like me and we aren't trying to impress one another :) That said, based on the size chart measurements I should have ordered a Large rather than the Medium that I ordered. I am 38-34-42 - I guess it was wishful thinking on my part. But I love the style and colors of this swimsuit - something different than the basic black. The padded bra feels supportive and helps to keep "headlights from shining" lol. And I love the look of boy-shorts - but I feel like this swimsuit emphasizes my already large thighs. I take really good care of my swimsuits especially the ones I wear to the gym because of the chlorine. I find that if I soak it and hand wash gently in a tub of water and white vinegar, it cleans great and it doesn't fade the fabric. So far the fabric has not "picked" and it doesn't feel like a cheap fabric, so I'm not concerned that it ever will. Bottom line, I really like the swimsuit - but I don't like how I look in it. (Good incentive for me to lose a few pounds!)

34 B/36A ordered both small and medium to try. First off the color is darker than pictured. Second and more problematic; these swim tops contain some sort of UPF treatment. Since there is no information on the tags as to how the UPF treatments are achieved I did not try them on. From my research a lot of UPF clothing is chemically treated with chemicals that may not be bodysafe. The desirable way is via the tightness of a fabric's weave and there are companies that do it this way. Since Amazon has chosen not to disclose how their UPF resistance is achieved I am returning these swim tops and can't recommend them due to body safety issues. This information should be in the product description so consumers can make an informed choice before purchasing. The versions I purchased were Amazon Prime with free returns and I am returning for free via UPS drop off.

I can’t get over how amazing this batting suit feels on. I absolutely HATE bathing suits and try to hide my mommy tummy with swim dresses. This one is so flattering and comfy! All of my Hilor, Miraclesuit, and la blanca suits are size Large, XL or 12-14. I was hesitant to get a smaller size, but the size chart showed I’d fit in a medium. IT DOES!! Go by your normal shirt size. I’m 5‘0“, 147 lbs, 36D. I’m afraid a large would be too big in the chest, but the torso might look better if it had some extra fabric. It fits me great everywhere else. There’s tons of booty coverage (even with the skirt flipped up) and my boobs aren’t spilling out. Even when it’s wet, it fits me like a glove! That’s another thing: it doesn’t look see-through when it’s wet! I love the asymmetrical skirt and the little tie, but make sure to tie it tight! Mine fell off in the hot tub. The material is soft and stretchy and has a nice ribbed texture. Definitely ordering another one. I can’t decide between green, orange or black. Black will hide more, so if you’re self-conscious of your tummy like me, get black. Hope this helps ☀️

I’m 5’6” and weigh about 145lbs. I was in between sizes. My waist was closer to the small but my hips at a medium. There’s already ruching so even if it’s a tad loose it still looks good in the waist. I was more concerned about having it too tight at my hips that it would’ve created a huge dent. I ordered the medium. Glad I did. :) I’ve put on some pounds after my last baby. Can’t lose it like I used to now that I’m in my 40’s. I’ve been having a hard time finding a swimsuit that makes me still feel young, that’s still age appropriate AND hides some of my problem areas. This swimsuit is a winner for me! The entire swimsuit is lined. The ribbed material along with the lining helps hide outlines of your private areas… you know exactly what I’m talking about! Ha! The ruching is flattering. It helps create a nice waistline and hides some unwanted rolls and belly button indentation. It comes with removable cups. I took the cups out. There was enough ruching and material to hide my nipples. The bottoms provide good coverage. The straps are adjustable. The side cinching area is 3” long. Cinched all the way up, the swimsuit is 2” higher on the sides. The side ties help camouflage denting of the hips. Very comfortable swimsuit. I have an average sized torso. This swimsuit has plenty of stretch and material with the ruching that I feel it would work great with longer torsos too. Great quality material and stitching. This has become my new fav and I plan to order a second color for this summer.

This swimsuit top is SO flattering and very adjustable- you put it on like a front closure bra and then tie it in the front as loose or as tight as you need. The straps are adjustable like bra straps. The coverage is just right for me, shows enough to be sexy but still covers everything it needs to. The front closure straps are nice because it pulls your boobs together for cleavage. I have 520cc high profile implants but a wide set chest/torso so I have great side boob but not much cleavage unless I have something pulling the girls together. The top feels secure, reasonably supportive especially because of how adjustable it is, and good quality. Hopefully it will hold up once worn. I didn’t love the bottoms. I was expecting a little bit higher cut on the sides because of the shape but that’s more of an illusion. It doesn’t come up any higher than any standard bikini bottom so if you’re looking for a higher cut suit that you can pull up over the love handles this isn’t it. It was also a bit strange because the back of the suit was relatively loose (my glutes measure 40” around so it’s not for lack of booty) while the front looked too small. There are elastic lines in the picture from my underwear but there are no elastic lines from the suit bottom itself. The brown color is true to product picture- a sort of faded muted brown, not a rich chocolatey color like I was hoping, but still looks fine. I’m keeping this suit because the top is fabulous. I’m going to order it in more colors to match other swim suit bottoms I have. For reference I ordered a medium- I’m 5’6” 155 lbs, 40” around glutes, 29”-30” waist currently, 32” under bust and 39” over bust measurements. I would suggest ordering based on your bottom size because the top is super adjustable and the bottoms are not. Hope this is !

Ok. I'm not one to post bikini pics publicly, BUT...when I say I'm impressed with CUPSHE bikinis, I...AM...IMPRESSED! This is the first time I've bought a bikini that's not a mix-n-match when it comes to sizing and I bought 2 from CUPSHE based on other reviews with photos/measurements, etc, and I love both! So, I hope I'm able to pay it forward... I'm 5'5", 117lb, more junk in the trunk than treasure in the chest, so I normally have to buy a size larger in bottoms. I went with a medium and it was perfect! (In both that I purchased!) I feel body-confident in it, it doesn't ride up as I move, it appears well-made (no poor stitching), no snags in the ribbed outer material, and the color is beautiful. I was worried the side cutouts would create a fleshy overflow, like some bras can, but not at all! The fit was perfectly secure without being constricting. Another small bonus is how the tags are attached. I'm a tag remover and these were perfect ~ attached by a string, rather than the tag directly being attached to the bikini itself. Easily removed with no itchy scratchy factor from leftover tag bits. It arrived with all tags attached, hygienic liner in place, and in a zipper type plastic bag. I definitely recommend CUPSHE and will probably be purchasing more myself soon!

This is a nice bathing suit that is very good quality, just as good as any expensive suit you would find at the big department stores. It fit me fine, however, I returned it due to the way it made me feel in it. I am searching for a one-piece bathing suit to have on hand for when we have family pool parties and I don't want to be too sexy in a bikini, and I love tie-dye so I thought this one was a good choice. One element that I loved about this suit is that the girth measurement was long enough. For those who don't know what "girth" is, it is the measurement from the tip of one shoulder seam, down diagonally across the body, ending at the crotch. It is the best way to tell if a swimsuit will not "ride up" and will feel comfortable on the torso. I am a ballet dancer and have purchased many leotards over the years and the dancewear manufacturers always tell the consumer what is the girth measurement. I do not understand why swimwear manufacturers do not do the same. It really is an essential piece of information that buyers need to know to make an accurate purchase. I am 5'3", an am currently on the heavy side at 140 lbs and I purchased a medium. I have a huge chest (32DDD), 29" waist, and muscular bubble butt, and although this suit supported my chest area just fine and held my boobs in pretty securely, and made my butt look okay (husband liked it), I decided to return it because it just felt too matronly. It has double layer fabric over the torso/stomach area that is shirred on the sides (gathered), and I did not like that. I do not have a tummy and my midsection is not my problem area, so while wearing it I felt like it just had too much fabric going on in that area. My problem area is holding up my giant boobs (all natural so they don't hold themselves up, unfortunately) and fitting my longer torso. I require at least a 52" girth measurement (a minimum of 26" from shoulder to crotch) and this was more than ample. This suit would work well for ladies that need the longer torso. I took a few measurements to help anyone with their decision making. For a one-piece, while it is very cute and very well-made, at my age I am not ready for a suit like this. Maybe when I am in my late 50s I will choose this style, but for now I am still on the hunt for a one-piece suit that has a bit more of a youthful vibe and is not so suited towards tummy control. This is just a personal preference. The swimsuit itself is very nice fabric, very soft, well-made, colorful, and I doubt that the tie-dye would fade.

Bought this rash guard for my wife and it's pretty much what you'd want for a day in the sun or a dip in the sea. At six feet tall and 190 lbs, finding swimwear that's not playing a game of "too short" with the sleeves is a challenge. This medium-sized top not only fits her snugly but also comes with the holy grail of long sleeves - thumb holes that she can actually use. 🌞 The material, while on the lighter side and a tad see-through, feels good on the skin. The designers were clever with the zipper - it's small but mighty, sewn in such a way that it tucks neatly under a fabric flap, ensuring her neck remains scratch-free. Plus points for that. The adjustable hem is a nice touch too, offering flexibility whether she wants it longer on the hips or shorter on the belly. 👍 On the downside, the material's transparency might not be everyone's cup of tea, especially if you're looking for more coverage. But overall, it's a worthy addition to the swimwear collection, and we're considering grabbing a few more in different colors. Solid 4.5/5 stars for this versatile piece.

Wow! This bikini is awesome! I wanted a high-neck top, but I also have a bit of underarm bulge, which makes this fit a hit-or-miss with many tops. For the price and free returns, I couldn't resist. I am so glad I tried it on. I bought a second suit because I like it so much. I am 5'5", 132#, 34C, and bought the Medium, which fits beautifully. The bottoms are really pushing it for my bum size and comfort level, but I'm going with it. The top has removable cups. First bikini I bought, they are perfect, second bikini, they are wrinkled. I could have tried an exchange, but I decided to buy replacement cups here at Amazon, and they work really well. I like that the bikini has seams to keep the cups in place but allowing for some adjustment--at least they won't overlap! One really great feature is the buckle in the back rather than a tie--I find this easier to put together. Unlike other tops I tried of this design, this bikini doesn't ride up--no fear of the girls dropping out, if you know what I mean. Packaging is nice in a thicker zip-lock bag that adds to the quality feel. Overall I am really happy with this suit.

I apologize for the up close picture but I wanted to show how it covers the pubic area! It doesn’t fully cover all hair if you haven’t shaved or waxed, but it’s better coverage than I’ve had in any other suit! The picture i posted of after running in place to see if it bunches! You can see a little bit of the texture of hair through the suit so you would need to be trimmed for the smoothest appearance but I’m fine with that! As for butt coverage there was a little hike in the seems after jogging in place, but I am still comfortable with it’s coverage. The only negative is that it doesn’t have phenomenal support of the chest area. It’s as good as any other swimsuit but I definitely wouldn’t run a marathon in it. I also am just at the max of the top size wise for a medium at 36C. The other thing I want to tell people is that the scrunched up but is only in the front. I’m guessing that’s to help with tummy control but if you look at it from the side can look a little jarring! Love the emerald color, love the style on me, and love the coverage! It’s nice to have coverage, but still be sexy.

I posted this in hopes it’ll help someone like me. I never post photo reviews lol. Typically, swimwear runs small. I have a lot of luxury brands and all that. But this is VERY true to size. I am 5’7 and currently 156 lbs and a 36DDD. I had a baby 7 months ago and do not breastfeed. I am about 15-20 pounds over my pre-pregnancy weight and felt insecure in all my other swimsuits. I ordered a medium and a large and the large was too big but was passable with some adjusting. I usually love swimwear that is double lined with no seams on the sides, especially the butt. I don’t hate this. The medium works perfectly and I would not advise sizing up unless you are heavy in the middle. I took the pads out because they always suck and I’m not too worried about nipple outlines haha. Who cares Lmao. Anyways, the ruched sides make the swimsuit very adjustable. I never thought I would like what I would normally consider a “cheap Amazon swimsuit” but this is awesome! I feel great!

The first thing I should make clear is that I am writing this review for three different purchases: the hot pink retro high waist, and the same set in white (I purchased after receiving the pink suit). And a third white bottom from another vendor for comparison.I am 5’2”, 137lbs, 55yo, athletic build with 34D/36C chest. I have things to hide in the abdomen and was seeking this retro high waist look, and a top with more coverage.I received the hot pink suit in a medium. The bust fit, but the bottoms didn’t appear to come up as high waisted as the photos. They are more below or at the belly button (and I’m short). The leg cut is also much higher than in the images. That said, I could pull it off and needed a suit fast and kept it, even though it clearly wasn’t as pictured. The material is fine but it doesn’t really offer tummy control.I decided to get one in white thinking that perhaps it was a fluke with the bottoms on the pink suit. I ordered the same size M in white. And don’t you know this white bottom that should have been exactly the same or better was even SMALLER in the rise than the pink bottoms. Not at all what I was expecting, and it was perplexing they would be different to what is pictured.I wanted the white bottoms as pictured so I looked at other places. Found a pair, the exact looking bottoms and ordered a pair. They ARE exactly like the picture on this page, but I didn’t get them here.So if you are looking for the true high waist retro bottoms (50’s) which are extremely flattering, it’s not what you will get with this page. The bottoms sent do not match what is pictured. However, it’s a good suit if the waist height doesn’t matter so much to you.My photos: the side leg difference between the pink and white bottoms in the exact same size, and the side leg/waist difference between the white bottoms sent by this company vs the white bottoms with the black tag I purchased elsewhere.

Size Medium. I’m 170lbs right now. 1st time trying this brand. Overall I like it and will keep it. I normally prefer cheeky cause my bum is so big lol. The BERLOOK one piece was a nice surprise. I did not realize form the picture it was actually velvet, which turned out to be soft and stretchy, and beautiful color. It looks equally nice before and after getting into the water. I took it on vacation, wore it a lot, especially for the evening swims or during a bit colder day. It looked very elegant and felt comfortable. Fits to size, for reference I am size 6-8 (Medium), 34DD in the bust, and short torso. The reason I took a star off is the lining. It is white, peeks put every time you turn. It is also adding the bulk to already thicker than usual and completely non-transparent fabric, and it has different elasticity too. What I did? I removed the lining and sewed the edges by hand. I am happy with the final outcome and wear the suit a lot. I am sure most people would not go that far. The straps are very long, but I do not see it as a problem rather an increase of options. My favorite look is crisscrossing on the back, then tying around the waist. I tried a few different versions. If you figured your favorite option – just cut the straps shorter, it won’t have any impact on the suit. I recommend this swimming suit, it is pretty and comfortable, looks dressy with a sarong.

Once it was wet it still held up and didn’t fit weird. The only thing I would mention is while holding my toddler the front would want to roll down a little since there isn’t a hard seam line around the edging. Aside from that would highly recommend. I’m 5’7” and 128lbs for reference and ordered the medium.

I'm an avid paddle-boarder and always on the lookout for swim bottoms that look good and function well and I think these are great 5'4"; measurements 39-28-40. I ordered a size Medium which was the recommended size by Amazon. I like the length of the rise. I like that they don't dig in at the top. I don't believe that they are see-through or CT inducing (they are lined with same color as the outside). I washed them in the sink and saw no sign of color bleed which is really good for an inexpensive swimsuit. Love the color - it's my go to for bottoms because it gives the illusion of a deeper tan. Slight rusching -- nothing crazy but better than the pair I tried with none at all. My only con is I wish there was a tiny bit more coverage to where it would actually cup the entire butt but I'll still confidently wear them in public. If you have a narrower butt these will likely do that. But it's hard to find truly full-coverage bottoms for myself and these are pretty close.

The colors on this suit are vibrant and fun, and a bright color like this complements [my] very pale skin quite well. The two-tone color combo gives two-piece vibes, but has the comfort and security of a one piece, which is exactly what I want while chasing my small children around a pool/beach and reminiscing about the summers I spent actually relaxing in a hot pink two piece ten years ago. In terms of appearance, this suit accentuates the chest, but comfortably holds everything in place, and the cutout shows off the best part of my abs- a well-developed two-pack, and downplays the post-childbirth-work-in-progress lower abs/stomach with some well-positioned ruching. I would give this 20 stars if I could, and I am contemplating buying a second one if, god forbid, something happens to mine and it is no longer available. It’s THAT good- so good I am including a picture of myself in said suit. If that’s not confidence, I don’t know what is. Don’t let the camera angles and natural light fool you; I’m 5’ 3” and 135-140 lbs.

This is the perfect swimsuit, especially if you just had a baby!! I’m 8 weeks postpartum so I wanted something to help disguise the pooch left over from carryin’ dat baby for 9 months! The flouncy top also helps disguise the fact that I’m a little lopsided right now bc of my slacker boob (if you breastfeed, you know what I’m talkin about!). I found this top pretty easy to nurse in as well, bc it’s pretty flexible and just loose enough. You can lift up the bottom to nurse, and then the flouncy material on top provides a little privacy. YALL. I have finally found my modest, yet youthful, mom suit! I’m a half inch over 5 foot, about 139 pounds, a 34DD, and ordered a medium for reference. I’m 32 years old, permanently look 6 months pregnant from my mommy pooch, and have been gaining weight, so I’ve outgrown all my old suits, sending me on a search for the holy grail of suits. We got a membership at the Y and frequent the beach, water parks, etc, so it was important to find one that was modest, yet let me keep up with my kids. Also, ladies with a large chest will understand how uncomfortable halter suits can be on the neck, and even with my being so short, one pieces with straps on the shoulders still feel like a tight fit. This is my first two piece since having kids and it gives me the same security my one pieces always have, JUST WAY MORE COMFORTABLE! I love that the bottoms come up to my belly button, and that the top has a band on bottom to avoid any mishaps when I’m active. Love the adjustable shoulder straps and my chest fit perfectly into the breast band. The bottoms are not full coverage, but modest enough. The freedom my shoulders and neck feel is priceless. Also, I was originally going to order a large since all my other mediums in other brands were too small, but I ordered a medium based off the measurement chart. I’m so glad I took the extra time to measure because a large would’ve been too big. The bottoms and top are snug and loose enough all at the same time. Literally perfect 👌 I haven’t been in water yet, but the material feels just as good and thick as any suit I’ve owned, so I’m confident it’ll be just as awesome in water. Love this suit, and will be back to order more in other prints!

I've never reviewed a swimsuit before but am doing so now because, at least out of the package, I love this one. This piece is definitely more comfortable than the other 3, slightly "sexier" styles I own. The straps are adjustable and wide enough to not cut in to the shoulders. The bust is comfortable on my 32G chest, with enough lift to not smoosh me in, sides high enough that I'm not spilling out, and an overall construction that holds "the girls" front and centre with a natural but not scandalous degree of cleavage. The sewn-in cups do not show. I don't notice any tummy control as advertised (hence the 4-star rating), but the shirring is flattering and brings out the waist-hips curves. The leg openings are low enough to cover my butt and stay put, without cutting in to the skin. I haven't worn this in the water yet but with the dark colors I'm not worried about see-through. Bonus: no horrible chemical smell like the other suits I've ordered online. For reference, I am 5'6", 135 lbs. 41-31-40, usually size 6/M. From the underbust seam to the leg opening on the side this suit measures 11" in the size M. I am high / shortwaisted but, not having a waistband, the torso also fits comfortable and looks nice. May order in another colorway... Edit: I have now worn this in the water; no transparency or stretching issues. Although this suit covers more skin than most of my others, it still got a "hubba hubba" out of the boyfriend :)

When I go swimming or playing in the ocean, I generally prefer a simple bathing suit. But there are times when you want to be in the sun or near the water and you want some coverage. This is exactly why I ordered the SHAPERIN Women's One Piece Skirted Swimsuit. It looks great out of the water! And the red is gorgeous.At 5'8" tall and 150lbs, finding swimwear that fits my torso and small bust can be a challenge. While the tummy control feature is a definite plus, I found that the padding in the bust was overwhelming. However, it's an easy fix - I removed the pads which resulted in a much more natural and comfortable fit. I got size medium and while it takes a tug or two to get it on, it fits snugly and isn’t too restricting. I would also have liked the legs to ride up higher on the thigh, but that’s just me!The material feels durable and holds up well with frequent use, which is essential for someone like me who spends most of the summer outside, preferably at the beach! It is machine wash and wear, though I line dry my swimming gear. This is very well constructed, with strong seams and reinforced stitching on the straps.This is a terrific swim dress, with or without the bra padding! I’m very happy with the silhouette and kicky skirt. I think the sizing is tricky and it’s best to go by the waist size, so if you think you might feel at all constricted by the tummy control, size up!

ZAFUL 2024 Women's Tankini Swimsuits Cut Out Crisscross Tankini Top with High Waisted Bottom Tummy Control Bathing Suit I got this suit for a beach vacation because I wanted to have some variety in suits. Sizing: My measurements are 39/30/37. I usually wear a medium or small. I got the medium, which was a little small for my bust but a little large for my hip measurements. That size feels like a decent fit. There are some issues in the fit of the top, which may be due to the cut and not to the size. What I like about the suit: The blue is a very nice bright blue color. The suit does look pretty cute and flattering. Although it says it has tummy control, it is not tight in any way. I think there’s some ruching along the abdomen to help with any pooch you may have. So keep in mind that this is not “Shapeware“. There are some awkward things about the suit, mainly to do with the top. First of all the style where it loops around your neck and ties in the back is very awkward to get on. I did figure it out, but I was not enjoying myself. Also the fit around my bust feels awkward. There’s what feels like a gap around the side of my bust that makes me concerned about coverage. I think if you are lounging poolside or at the beach, you’re not going to worry about coverage. However, if I’m in the ocean, I will be checking the coverage of this suit before I emerge. Also, the pads on the bust are not removable and I wish they were. They are very small almost like they are made only for nipple coverage, but it’s really hard to get them in the right place so that they don’t look awkward. It is possible that this whole get up might be more attractive and fit better for larger busted women. A lot of my bust measurement reflects a large rib cage. Overall, I think the suit could work for some people. It’s cute and attractive. And I love the tankini style overall. I feel like the top could be hit or miss. So I’m going to give this a cautious recommendation – that you might like it, but you might want to return it.

I never leave reviews but this suit is so amazing I just had to. I’m a 71 year, old 5’4”, 131 pound grandmother with a long torso. I wanted a flattering suit for my back yard pool and for our upcoming trip to the beach. I purchased size medium / 6-8. Having a long torso is a problem with one piece suits. If I don’t get a long torso suit, it pulls on my shoulders and my “cheeks” show. I read the reviews and because both short and tall women were raving about this suit I thought I’d give it a try. It arrived quickly and on time. I put it on right away and think it fits and looks great! Not going for sexy at my age-just fit, modest, comfortable, and flattering. I was looking at another company whose prices were exorbitant because after all swimsuits wear out very fast because of chlorine exposure so to have to pay a lot of money for a suit is a waste. The bra is wonderful. I need a lot of support and this suit delivers. The color is what it looked like on the Amazon app and it’s very pretty. I haven’t gone in the pool yet but will very soon. I’m just so excited I had to say how happy I am with this suit! One slight negative is the boy short bottom is slightly loose in the back and a little long in the front but the skirt is long enough to cover that little faux pas!

I absolutely love the color of this suit. The material is standard- the level of quality you would expect from say a department store but not enough lycra for a high end suit. For this price point, I think it’s pretty good. The suit is lined in the front only. The lining is completely closed. It has those circle shaped foam pads you find in all sports bras but they are not removable as there are no slits in the inside liner. They’re not removable, but they are mobile within the liner - just free floating in there. I think it would’ve been better to sew them in place so you don’t have a problem with them getting folded creased in the washing machine and then not laying right. I’ll probably tack these in place before I wear it. I am 5’ 7” and 140 lbs. I got a medium. The bottom fits pretty good. The top feels just a little bit small and has a tendency to pull up when I raise my arms over my head. I am small chested- 36 B so it surprised me the top felt short. It just feels a little too short on my rib cage. If you’re someone who very petite, this will probably fit you better. I think it would be a bit, short/revealing for someone who has a very long torso in that it would slide up farther than you’d want it, and it would be a little difficult for someone with a very large bust because they would be spilling out the top. Who knows that may be the fit you’re going for. The straps are NOT adjustable so the fit it comes with is what you get. The bottoms are “cheeky” in that they fit like a french cut bikini brief with a little cheek visible on the sides but I didn’t have any problems with the suit riding up into a wedgie. Stayed in place well even when walking, bending, climbing stairs, ect… For me, I wanted something athletic that gave me the support of a sports bra that I could wear hiking and then be able to swim at the top of the hike. I think this will work really well for that.

I am the type of person who would never dare post a picture/review for bathing suits, but after receiving these I had to write one because WOW. I know how hard it is to do bathing suit shopping online and pictures help a lot but I feel like my body type is the least I can find reviews about. I had a hard time deciding what size to buy so I ordered 2 pairs in a size small and medium. I am small chested up top, small on the bottom, no hips, no waist, and I have long legs for being short. For guidance I am 5’2 , 126 Lbs, 34A bra size, 31inches on my waist , and 38 inches on my hip. As you can see my body type just varies on the given size chart . I can be a size 4-6, 6-8, small, or medium. It’s hard for me. For bathing suits I normally need a medium bottom, and small top . Surprisingly the size small was way too tight up top, so I tried the medium set and it fit PERFECT! It’s a little tight up top still, but I prefer it that way so I don’t have to worry about my top falling off . I gained a lot of weight and have been working out to lose it . My fat normally just goes to the back of my thighs and my sides giving me love handles. The bathing suit bottom kind of acts like a tummy tuck for my love handles lol. Also, the bottoms are a little cheeky and for someone like me who is small on the bottom, it still works! I still have cellulite that shows, but this bathing suit makes me not care about that. I feel good and comfortable ! I finally found a good bathing suit and I’m going to order so many more of these in different colors! P.S Both bathing suits in the pictures are the size Medium(:

Yes, I am that person who looks through every pictured review. I am the investigative type when it comes to online shopping—I’m always looking to find a person with the same build as me so I could see a more practical review. I originally got the white and black polka dot and when it came in and I tried it on, I immediately went and ordered this one. I receive compliments every time I wear either one and a few friends have even went and bought the same suit (all different body types, all slaying it!). I like this top. It’s pretty true to size (got a medium and I’m a 34DD, so usually medium). I was worried it would be a bit see-through, but seems to be not see through at least when dry. Seems of decent quality - I wouldn’t be diving in it, but perfect for the beach or lounging by the pool.

I liked the quality for the price. I wanted a swim top that wouldn’t leave strap tan-lines so the removable straps are nice. I’m 5’8, 155lbs, 38C cup, medium build with average length torso and a medium seems the right fit, not too tight nor too loose. The strap claps seem durable and the clasp areas are sewn well. The criticisms I have are that the “ladies” didn’t have any lift which I prefer at least a little bit of in a suit. And the cup liners are sewn in so I cannot add/put in cup liners to help boost. But some people may prefer the liners to be sewn in. With the straps on and tightened you may get a little lift but not much and I bought for the purpose of not having the straps on. I have not worn it in a pool yet so can’t speak about that but may come back to update. I’m keeping and may try to find cups I can just place between the inside of the suit and me because it seems secure enough on me that the cups wouldn’t fall out. It is very flattering through the torso though and love the white. Probably would only wear to the pool and not a beach or lake though as I could foresee it turning off-white/getting stained fairly quickly based on the material.

I really like the quality of this coverup (the material looks and feels great), but as other reviewers have mentioned, it runs quite large. I tried this on today, I WISH I had taken a photo. To not have an underwire, this top is incredible. I am a 34C, but was previously a 38DD. After going from 228lbs to 134lbs over the last few years, and having and breastfeeding two kids, the girls don’t exactly stand up anymore. With many tops, these things fall down like a broken miniblind lol. You would NEVER know it while I’m wearing this top. The fit is so flattering. It offers full coverage AND support with no underwire! The straps are easy to adjust, and also very flattering. I can’t attest to how much support would be offered to my much larger chested ladies, as you can’t really adjust the back much, and the shoulders only go so far. But when I tell you this thing is comfortable, my goodness. This top checks all my boxes of what I look for in a top: no underarm flab, support, no boobs falling out, and extreme comfort. I’m on my way to buy another in a different color right now! I will try to add a photo in this review later today! Edit to add: it is quite the challenge taking a photo by yourself. Dont question it, just buy the top. I’ve already ordered 2 other colors.

LOVE this top. For reference, I ordered a Medium and I'm 5'3", 135lbs, 32D/DD, 27" waist, 40" hips. My only compliant is that it doesn't come in more colors.

I’m still going to keep this bikini because I like the top and I’m still losing weight so maybe soon the bottoms will fit.

Long review for people who want to know more about the fit of the product. I bought a size medium and it fits okay. I am 5’5 and 145 lbs. I had a baby a year ago and have a little bit extra weight below my belly button now. I usually wear a size 8-10 in pants and am a 34DD in bras. Overall the fit of both the top and bottom is reasonable for the price. The straps to the top are adjustable, but there are no hooks to adjust the width of the top. fortunately for me, the width fit me. The breast pads are not sewn in place and do not fill the entire cup. They are larger than nipple pasties and you can move them around a little, but I don’t think they are removable. According to the size chart I should have gotten a large, but I think the band width would’ve been too big for me if I did. It fits snug in the cups, but I’m okay with it. I think the medium top would work best for those who typically wear are a 34C-D or a 36B-36D. Because the band size isn’t adjustable I think it would ride up too high on someone who wears a 32 or smaller and be too tight for someone who wears a 38 or larger. The bottoms were actually smaller than I thought they would be. The elastic around the waist band and legs cuts into my skin just slightly, but I don’t think this is a fault in the design. I think my body just doesn’t fit into them that well. They aren’t the best fit for me, but they do hold everything in. I think the size medium shorts would work best for someone who is a size 6-8 rather than a 8-10. Maybe they would fit better on someone who is a little taller as well? I am not sure. I wish they would have sold the top and bottom separately, but for the price I think I will just buy a different pair of bottoms to go with it and if for whatever reason I lose 5lbs they might fit better.

Sizing: After looking at the picture of this swimsuit on the model I HAD to get it. I read the reviews on sizing and they are very conflicting. I ignored the advice to size up and am so glad I did. The swimsuit fit perfectly with a little wiggle room in the bottom. The top was a tad snug, but I wanted it that way so that it doesn’t slip down while I’m walking. My weight fluctuates quite a bit so I will put my current measurements down as well as my heavier measurements (it has fit me through all of these sizes). Measurements: Height: 5’5” Weight: 125-135lbs Waist: 26-28in (smallest part of waist) Hips: 36-38.5in Chest: 32C Build: Athletic with weight mostly in thighs Fit: The bottoms are not as high waisted as advertised which I’m not a huge fan of, but I do like the front coverage they provide. The back is cheeky, but I actually felt comfortable with it because of how full coverage the front is. (If you want full coverage bottoms, front and back, these are not the ones lol). I think they run true to size IF your bra size is on the size chart. (Just the bra size, the rest of the chart is inaccurate so I would take it as a mild suggestion). If you are very chesty or like to wear your clothes tighter, size up. Material: The material is excellent quality. Thick enough to feel secure, but soft/absorbent enough for the water. I rated the swimsuit 4 stars because my first swimsuit from this seller came with a wire sticking up out of the side. It was sharp and poked me when I was wearing it. Amazon fixed this super quickly (2 days) and sent me another one that did not have that issue. Therefore, I don’t know if I had a fluke or if the quality control is lacking. Regardless, I would buy the swimsuit again.

Loooove this top and the comfort it provided. Highly recommend! I ordered a Small and am 120lbs, 5'2", 32B. The fabric was nice and soft and stretchy, and I loved that it went down my torso a little further than some (if the knot was looser). Nice that it's adjustable in that way and I could tighten the knot, or completely tuck it under for a different look. The buttons are functional, which is also cool. Cups are removable, or you could swap them out for something else. Accidental sunscreen on the suit did not stain is at all! After a few wears I noticed a few negatives: 1. There was some weird itchy patch on the inside of the suit near the buttons, so that rubbed against my skin. More washes might fix that. 2. The suit stretched out after a few days of swimming. 3. The cups slid around a bit and needed to be adjusted throughout the day. Might not be an issue for bigger chests!

This is the second bathing suit I purchased. The first arrived size med and had stretched out exposed stitches (I’m guessing someone returned it) I also returned it and bought another one because other than that I loved the suit. I purchased a small the second time around though as the first one left me feeling that if I lose the baby weight it would not fit. The busy area was also extremely large for a medium and even though I could tie it tighter to fit correctly (natural d cup but I’ve breast fed so they are not as perky as they use to be 🤷🏻‍♀️😂). The small arrived and I tried it on over underwear (you can see them in the picture so please disregard how the fabric shows by my leg it is not bathing suit lining) this time everything is perfect including the fit EXCEPT this bathing suit has completely different gold metal accents/embellishment than what is pictured and what I previously received. As you can see in the picture it is textured and I really don’t like the look of it. I can easily remove them but I like the look of the little gold accents. I will update this review once the problem is resolved, the seller has already reached out to me to fix the issue. Other than that I love this bathing suit. It makes my baby tummy look good and I feel great in it without it being too sexy or having any nip slips. For reference I’m 5’5” 145-150 pounds bigger thighs and bottom, baby belly and a 36D. (Size 7/8 pants, medium top and bottoms). I did include the only picture I have of the medium, it’s the one with the white stitches showing. I know it’s a lot of info but I super appreciate when people post this kind of i formation, buying online is hard for sizing even with size charts.

WOW! This swimsuit is amazing! I’m 5’4” 135-138 lbs (size 4 jeans, size small tops) and I ordered size small. It fits perfectly except the cups are a little big. I am a 34B so I’m not surprised. I don’t think you can tell there’s a little extra room in the cups when I’m wearing it, so I’m not too bothered. Overall this swimsuit is great quality. I normally order more expensive swimsuits from Nordstrom so I’m shocked this swimsuit looks and feels so much more expensive than it is. The material is thick, comfy and stretchy. It looks like something you would buy from a higher end store. This swimsuit definitely has some tummy control and holds you in. I just had a baby a few years ago and this swimsuit makes me feel great. I am a 31 year old mom so I wanted a one piece that is classy, just a little sexy but still looks youthful. I’m at the stage of life where I want to look a little more appropriate for family beach/pool trips but I don’t want to lose the sexy/cute factor. I definitely think this swimsuit hits the nail on the head. I wish I would’ve ordered a fun color, but I didn’t realize how flattering this would be. I’m definitely going to be ordering more colors. I’m probably going to order the leopard or bright blue next. Wow I’m just extremely impressed! Most one piece swimsuits don’t really give the feeling of holding you in but this one certainly does. I also love that the cut on the legs isn’t too low, because that’s very unflattering on my pear shape. I can’t stand when the leg holes (for lack of a better word) come down really low and cut your legs/hips off. I like the leg cut to be a slightly higher cut, but not too high. The bum coverage is a solid medium coverage in my opinion. Not too cheeky to where your cheeks are all over the place but not too full coverage to where it looks frumpy. The swimsuit also stays in place really well all over. It doesn’t shift around and give you wedgies lol. I am sometimes a small or medium in swimsuits. I took a wild guess and ordered a small. I would say if you are between sizes you could size down based on my experience. If you have a large bust size I really think you will love this swimsuit. Like I mentioned there was room left in the cups for me so I think well endowed ladies will appreciate the cups aren’t super small. The cups are also more structured and substantial. The ruching is on the front AND back of the swimsuit, that is something you don’t often find. I’m not always a fan of ruching because I think if it’s done wrong it can actually make you look pregnant. This ruching is done RIGHT. It’s very flattering. If you’ve had kids or have a little extra in the tummy area, buy this suit!! I think a lot of different body types will work with this swimsuit. I am a pear shape but I also think apple, straight or hourglass would look fantastic in this. It’s kind of a universally flattering swimsuit in my opinion. No matter what your age, size or shape I can imagine this being a winner. The little gold accents on the straps are a cute touch. In these pictures I still had the plastic on the gold parts but since these pictures I’ve worn the swimsuit and taken them off. It came well packaged in a plastic bag and with a protective liner in the crotch. I don’t really have much else to say other than BUY IT! I purchased a sheer sarong to go with it and it looks fabulous. FYI- I purchased this swimsuit with my own hard earned money and this is a genuine review of a mom who found a flattering swimsuit! :) Update: I ordered the leopard suit in the over the shoulder strap style also. It’s the other style on this listing that is not a halter. I love it as well, but I think I like the halter style better! I’m buying another color in the halter style next.

This suit is super bright green, fits well and feels secure like it won't fall down. I got a small (32C, 130lbs, 5'6"). The fabric is double layer with padding, completely opaque. I thought it would be higher waisted than it is, so the bottoms aren't quite as flattering around my stomach as I'd like. They are definitely cheeky, but not uncomfortably showy. A weird thing to note: don't wear this suit in the hot tub if you're trying to impress anyone... For some reason air gets trapped between the fabric layers so it balloons out and looks bizarre. Also, I only rinsed the suit before wearing and the dye ran, nearly ruining a white towel, so make sure you thoroughly wash/dry before wearing (it didn't shrink in the dryer). Despite all that, I'm really happy with this swimsuit for the price and will be wearing frequently.

I only write reviews if the product was horrible, or I feel like people need to know how awesome the product really is. I’m an avid Amazon customer & rely heavily on authentic reviews. I also am obsessed with bathing suits, so I’ve purchased very expensive ones and very inexpensive ones (such as this one!). I’ve tried other bathing suit sellers on Amazon due to the amazing prices, but this is the first bathing suit I can honestly say I love. In the past, the Amazon bikinis I’ve purchased I wore only once, or absolutely hated the fabric & make of the suit. For example, I’ll never buy a Zaful suit again. 😳 I noticed that this suit had various reviews from all types of body shapes & most say that it is a great buy. I 100% agree that all women should give this suit a try! + Positives and - Negatives: + I rated this bikini a 4/5 only because I am comparing it to all bikinis I’ve ever purchased. Therefore, for the actual price & quality of the suit, I’d say it’s 5/5. + There is NO filter on my photo only REALLY good natural lighting. This photo shows an honest depiction of the color. I think it is very flattering. I have a medium complexion. + Fits and snugs in all the right places while also making me feel comfortable enough to run around on a beach, however.... - I am right on the edge of 5’6” and 130ish lbs, so I decided to buy a medium because I tend to squeeze into my bottoms when I buy a small with Amazon. Due to this, my bottom is a little baggy in the back, and may ride up at times but it isn’t a big deal to me. I’ve had worse. + Right after I got this suit, I purchased a small in the red. I’ll update this if I think the small is too small for me! Gist: This suit is flattering on many body types. Get it!

I initially purchased this suit in pink but when I opened the package there was an inch long tear in the seam on the left side so I returned it and got the blue one, which was not damaged. (I needed it ASAP and the pink one would not have arrived in time.) I used it for an 80s themed workout at our gym and it was perfect for the aesthetic. This is a bathing suit and not a leotard, so the fabric was lined and a little thicker than I expected. The top is padded but not too much. It helped with shaping and made it so that I didn’t have to wear a bra (although my friend who is more endowed than I am wore the same suit and needed to add a bra — it still looked great even with the bra). The back is open and the butt coverage is reasonable: a little cheeky but not anything like the typical 80s leotard thong. I wore leggings underneath and it didn’t look bulky and the seams didn’t show through. My only complaint is that the straps were prone to slipping but I did complete an hour long HIIT workout and it didn’t fall off, so that’s a win! I got pretty sweaty during the workout but very little sweat showed through the suit. I got a size small and it was perfect. I’m 5’8”, 135lbs and an A cup. I would have given a 4.5 star rating if I could since the only real issue was the initial tear and the straps.

Since swimsuit shopping trips can be traumatic and have never purchased a swimsuit on line before, I was concerned about sizing and the way the swimsuit would look on my body type. But this is a nice suit! I am a 34C, a size 8, 5’0” tall (barely) and 120 pounds. Based on the size chart, I would have been a size small in the bust and a medium in the waist and hips. I read a lot of reviews and decided to purchase the small and it fits perfectly. So based on my experience, if you are in-between sizes, I would advise to buy a little small. This swim suit has bright colors and I love the tropical vibe to it. However, it’s probably not the best style for my body type. I am very short and the “blousy” top style tends to “pull me down” in height and so is probably better suited to someone taller than me. The back is flattering and has a good amount of coverage over the behind. You can wear it a couple of different ways that actually look better for my short stature. The black elastic straps (which are adjustable) can be completely removed and the “flouncy” top pulled up over the shoulders more like a regular shirt. It tends to pull the look “up” and not make me look so short and compacted. I like the suit a lot. The bright colors, overall fit, and the price make it a keeper, even though it’s probably not the perfect style for my body type.

Perfect swimsuit for anyone who wants a smidge more modesty but still wants to look cute! After having a baby and weight loss in my mid-30’s I was looking for something that would cover a little bit more but didn’t look matronly and this suit checks all the boxes. I feel like I can comfortably play in the water and sand with my toddler without any cheeks or peeks faux pas. I’m pretty petite at 4’10” 110lbs and purchased a size small in army green. I took my photos in midday sunlight without any indoor lights on and feel like the color is accurate. With the adjustable straps fully tightened, I feel completely secure up top (I’m a 34C bra size). The skirt hits at the perfect length, something I was worried about being too long since I’m so short, and the skirt being built in will be great so there won’t be any fussing to readjust it with movement and getting wet. I honestly didn’t have high expectations for a random swimsuit from Amazon but I can confidently say that I’m extremely impressed. The structure and seams feel well constructed so I have high hopes this swimsuit will hold up for a long while. I washed it and hung to dry after taking these photos and it held up fine. I’ll update my review after a few wears and putting it to the test but I suspect that won’t be needed. 10/10. Do it, buy the swimsuit.

Background: It's a cute one-piece. I didn't look at the one-star reviews before I bought it. I hope my bathing suit doesn't break once it's washed like the other reviewers. I won't be wearing this for a few weeks. I bought it for a last-minute bathing suit for my trip to Puerto Rico. Target and Walmart are a hassle due to batting suits being too big on me for try-ons or trouble finding smalls and matches. So I went to amazon. How it fits me: Depending on the brand I'm an xs-s. I'm 5' 3, and 100 or so pounds. The bottom had fitted more tightly than I'd like it (my buttcheeks are squished by the lining, and I was going to wear another bathing bottom underneath (period bathing bottom), so I could swim (I hate tampons)). The waist and the chest area of the bathing suit are looser than I would like them to be. I really think this bathing suit is more so for a certain type of body shape by the way it fitted me. (Also, I'm a small-chested woman, A-B cup depending on the brand). I think it'll maybe best fit a C-cup with a smaller bottom for a size small. (But the loose fit doesn't bother me all that much and isn't that bad in my opinion. I do wish it would hug me tighter though.). Overall: It's cute <3 And isn't all that bad for me so I'm going to keep it. But if you read my post, just keep in mind the downsides. Do I recommend buying it? Yes and no depending on your body shape, rather you like the fit and I still don't know if mine will rip if it's washed. Recommendation star: 3.5 (This is a long post, but I'm also bored playing Ow2)

I am 5’8” and 135 pounds for reference. Bra size 32C (or 34B in some brands). I’m 42 years old and I’ve had three c-sections—with a poochy lower belly scar to show for them now. Suits with tummy control and higher waisted bottoms are key. This suit has both of those, looks good and covers all the key spots without looking either too frumpy or too revealing. Score. I initially got this suit in red in size medium, and I still have that one and wear it on occasion. It works fine actually. The small is just a better fit for my shape. The medium is a little large in the top (particularly in the shoulders, and upper boob area) and has a little too much extra material in the front ruching on the bottoms. Still cute and perfectly wearable at the pool with the kids and whatnot. Just not as flattering as the small in my case. I’d love something that could magically flatten my post baby x 3 belly completely, but this one does a good enough job for me. It controls and hides the c-section scar area very well. And the fluttery top adds nice emphasis up there, and draws the eyes away from any hint of poochy business below, so that’s a plus for sure. It’s a great suit overall. Sexy without being over the top in any way. Comfortable too. Yay. Pics of size small black suit in first three images. Last is pic of the red in medium, mainly showing the extra material in the top.

I am a 36 year old mom of two and just wasn't feeling great about wearing a bikini this summer. That being said, I still wanted something cute. I am 5' 2" and weigh 120 lbs. I ordered a small and it fits well and is comfortable. When the straps are removed, you can wear the ruffled part off the shoulder or pulled up on the shoulder; both ways look cute. The padding in the chest is also removable. The first time I wore the swimming suit was to simply try it on and I really liked it. My issue with the suit is that the second time I put it on, I was out of town and going to a pool with friends. The loop that the strap hooks into came off as I was putting the suit on. This was the only only bathing suit I brought with me so I just removed the straps. Thankfully, the suit was still comfortable and looked good. It's a small fix; I just don't expect to be sewing things back together on a brand new suit. Other than that, the suit seems well made...maybe it was just a fluke?

I ordered a size small which I'd say was an accidentally ideal fit for my 5'5 130 lb self. The top seems to run smaller than the bottom. I very much liked the length of the top; I think I'm "long torso" and this was perfect for that. If you typically find that tankini tops are prone to being shorter than you like, this one works well and as I tried it on (in my room, because it's January in the midAtlantic here) it never "rode up." The bottom skirt is longer than some I own and offers plenty of coverage....I love the modest look but I think it's almost too long for me. Or maybe it was just that the skirt is more "full" and kinda more "flouncy" than I was expecting. Both the top and the bottom have a lot of coverage and overlap. and the waist of the skirt was a fine fit for me (usually I wear a 6 bottoms these days...with a few brands I can squeeze into a 4, for perspective). The star deduction is for an oddity that may or may not be a one-off; The top and bottom seem to be just ever so slightly different fabric "sheen" levels....like a slightly different fabric all together. The top I loved, it's a thick black matte fabric that looks higher end and is of very good quality. The bottom, however, is my star deduction as the fabric seemed different, more thin and a bit more shiny/sheen than the top. I didn't like the bottom as well as the top, but I don't know how noticeable it would be to any onlookers, in the water, or even to other buyers, but I was looking carefully at the item for the purposes of the review and might be heavy on the scrutiny here. Nice suit overall, and the nice thing about tankinis of course is that the pieces can be mixed and matched, so I'll probably do that as I prefer the top but others might feel the opposite! I think bathing suits are just tough all around so 4 stars here is a win :-]

When I decided to return to swimming to help with back pain, I wanted a practical suit that was comfortable yet still stylish. I wanted a suit with thicker shoulder straps, not too low cut and with full coverage in the rear. Additionally I am happy with the amount of padding in the upper portion. It is thick enough for privacy, and it does not shift when the suit is washed.For reference to help other petite shoppers, I'll tell you about my size and how the suit fits me. I am 5' 2" and 100 lbs. I am 34"-28"-36.5" and normally wear size 6-8 clothing. I decided on the size small bathing suit, and I am glad I did. It fits very well without riding up or having the straps either dig into my shoulders or slide off. Unlike others, I have not had any issues with the metal buckles bothering me. I swim for an hour a week, and I have had no irritation from the suit. When it is dry, of course it is a little snug, but once it's wet it fits perfectly.As an added bonus, I found that I can use the decorative band between the cups to attach my locker key. I added a carabiner, then hook my key to it and flip everything inside the suit.I've been wearing this swim suit for the past seven months, and so far the colors have not faded, and the suit has only stretched minimally in the outer, ruched layer. I am so pleased with this bathing suit that if it I were to ever need another, I would order this style again. Based on my experience, I would recommend it to anyone needing a serious swim suit or something beautiful for a cruise. It's just a wonderful garment.

I ordered a variation of sizes/colors of this bathing suit to compare them. I ordered a small blue and a medium orange from this seller to see which size/color I liked better. I also ordered a small orange from another seller (Aleumdr) which was the more expensive one. The orange colors were different between the two sellers, this brand was more of a faded orange and the Aleumdr was bright vivid orange. Also there is a small difference in the flower print, the ruffles and this brand (RXRXCOCO) does not have ruffles on the back of the bottom while Aleumdr suit does. The more expensive brand (Aleumdr) does seem like a better quality but RXRXCOCO suit is great quality too. For the bathing suit itself, it’s very cute and a good fit. The medium was too big for me and baggy. I am 34C, 5’1 and 100 lb, normally wear a small, and the small fit me perfectly. Medium was definitely too big on the bottom, I could’ve kept the medium too without it falling off since it’s stretchy. I have a shorter torso so the suit pretty much doesn’t show any midriff area for me. It’s comfortable and doesn’t fall down even without the straps. I would recommend this suit!

I love this beautiful blue one piece swimsuit. The color is fabulous. The material is on the heavier side but not too thick. It’s not see through. It is a little trying to get out of when wet, the zipper opening is short, which could be an issue. The built in shorts have a 3” inseam, the skirt is built into the suit and covers the built in shorts. The sleeves have a cuff like end so they stay down and there are thumb holes. The sleeves are tighter fitting and really can’t be rolled up. I did roll them once over the thumb hole so they didn’t look long, but you can’t push them up over your elbow. There is a built in bra and the pads are removable. I removed them because they were located too high for me:0 There is plenty of room in the small size for a bigger chest by removing the pads. I am 5’5”, 118lbs, 34B wearing a small. It fits well but still has bust room with the pads inserted. I have a long torso so I feel the built in bra is a little high but removing the pads helped. This suit offers great sun protection but I do wish it was offered in short sleeves. I get a little too hot in these long sleeves if I’m not in the water. Once in the water or after you get out of the water it’s very cooling. There are two pockets on the outside. Overall, I think it fits me well, and it’s stylish. I feel the busy blue leaf color combo pattern would be forgiving to most figures and recommend if you are looking for a one piece long sleeve swimsuit. It’s really stunning and I get so many compliments when I wear it.

This basically looks like a sports bra, nothing really special, a very basic style. However, it's pretty well done for what it is. The fit is nice - I'm 5', 115lbs, normally a 32/34A and the small fits as expected. I like that the top is cut a little higher and everything is adjustable. I was able to get a comfortable yet snug fit so that I don't have to worry about anything hanging out when I'm with my kids (while I'm bending, lifting, etc). The seams all seem sturdy, the hardware is all metal, and there are small pieces of boning on either side of the bra part so it won't bunch up. I like having some padding, so I like that there are pockets for removeable cups, but the included ones are the cheap thin ones that fold and bunch. I've bought higher quality cups for other bathing suits and bralettes and it makes a world of difference, so will probably do that here. I removed the cups and washed the top in the regular laundry and it came out perfect. Overall, a good top, but I'd recommend looking for a discount.

Top: the top fits well for me, and I appreciate it as decent coverage as it made it much more comfortable to be active in it. The only complaints I have is that due to being halter top. It can sag down on the string more than I like (pictured) which means it has to be adjusted by pulling it wider, but I won’t blame the suit for that one. The padding in it is typical, and I didn’t have any problems with getting nippy. I liked that it was backless and the halter strings were long, which was ideal Bottoms: these bottoms are really comfortable, however, the problems that I have with them is that the lining of the inside of suit is white, and I bought it in the orange color, so the shorts roll up and look something like white underwear poking out (pictured). I really wish it was the same material, it would have gotten 5 stars with that fixed. Overall, I bought this suit specifically for the bottoms, because I did not want to maintain a bikini line and even with the shorts rolling up I was pretty well covered. Ties: the ties on the suit make it really adjustable which I appreciate. Each tie is functional, not just decorative and so it makes more customizable to what fit you like. I really like the ones on the bottoms, really helps my butt look less flat, which is an issue with higher waisted bottoms. The ties make the suit more finicky overall, especially the first couple wears but once they’re tight they will stay for the most part, even while active. Fit: I am a 5’9” person with wide shoulders and wear a 34c bra, and I sometimes wear size small tops but am always safe sizing up to a medium. Typically I am a dress size 6 but occasionally a 4 and size 6 jeans and wear medium size underwear. I bought a size small and this fit me very well, definitely glad I bought the size recommended to me, it was very accurate.

My preteen daughter is 5'0" and 100# with a developing body. So, she wanted a modest swimsuit with a built-in bra and lined cups, which are not available in kid-sized swimsuits. So, I got this swimsuit in a small--which fits a little long in length but fits in the body very well. It is easy to take up the straps a little with some simple sewing to accommodate her height. The built-in cups are not too shaped, so she's not sticking out in places she doesn't have, but it provides the coverage we were looking for. The colors are great for a young girl, and the style is surprisingly fun. She actually was smiling ear to ear trying it on. The style will also allow her to play at the water park without worrying about any malfunctions. The fabric is thick, less stretchy than her other swimsuits. The straps are not adjustable, so that may be an issue for some. Easy to put on, just step in and pull up. I highly recommend this swimsuit.

I'm 5'3" 125 pounds and ordered this swimsuit in my usual size small after referring to the seller's size guide. The sizing is accurate, and the suit fits me well. It seems to be well made, there are no loose threads or defects that I can see. This swimsuit is comfortable to wear, it doesn't dig in anywhere or ride up. The material is smooth. The swimsuit is a great length, too. I'm petite, so often swimsuits are too long, making them bag in the bum, but this one doesn't! This suit is very flattering, it smooths out my tummy lumps and bumps, and completely hides my love handles. I have a very small bust (32B) but this suit kind of emphasizes it and makes me look curvier than I am in that area (YAY!). There isn't much chest support in this suit, so it's probably best suited to small-chested people like myself. I'm really pleased with this suit! I haven't actually gone swimming in this suit yet but I have been using it in my outdoor hottub and just hand-washing it afterwards, and it's holding up great. This swimsuit is great - it's comfortable and it's flattering.

I have had this on my wishlist for a year or so & it finally went on sale during the spring event. I am 5’5”, 125 lbs, 34B, with a little tummy pooch. The small fit me perfectly! I got this because I wanted a swimsuit that covered my middle for SUP boarding (one less place to apply sunscreen), I love purple, and this was the first one-piece swimsuit I came across that looked cute AND had shorts!! For a few reasons, I only wear shorts swimsuit bottoms, so when I saw this one had that under the skirt, I was sold. It looks adorable IMO (and I've gotten compliments on it). I was glad to find the seam on the shorts is just one horizontal seam further back that isn't very noticeable--I've had some shorts bottoms with bulky, irritating seams in obnoxious places. I wasn't quite sure if I'd need a small or medium, but when I ordered, the purple was only in a small, so I bought that and hoped for the best. I'm 5'8.5" with a long torso, about 122 lbs, and wear a 34D bra. The small is almost perfect--so I suspect the medium would have been a little too big. The top edge of the bust is the tiniest bit tight, but not enough to bother me while paddling on a SUP board or cause any chafing. The only thing is I had to adjust the straps to the the longest they could go, and the shorts still try to ride up my crack. However, I've been stretching the straps gently which is helping, and when the bottom is wet (I haven't gotten the entire suit wet yet), it does weigh down/stretch the suit just a tiny bit which helps it not ride up. However, if I were any taller (or possibly, the same height but slightly thicker), the small wouldn't have worked.

I found these bottoms to be comfortable and well-made, especially considering the low price point. But I do think they run a little small. I'm 5'8" and 118 pounds at the moment, with a 26" waist and 36" hips, placing me on the smaller end of a size small according to the sizing chart. The size small feels a bit snug over my hips and creates a little muffin top where there wasn't one before, especially from the back (see photo). My weight fluctuates and I don't think these will be comfortable anymore when I inevitably gain 5-10 pounds. For now they're fine. I think these bottoms would fit best on women who, unlike me, do not have a long torso and/or aren't over 5'7". I know they're intended to be low-rise but if they were any lower I'd be showing crack. And "full back coverage" is a relative concept. These cover more of my butt than many other options but they cannot fully contain my cheeks-- and I have minimal booty at the moment! I ordered a few bikini bottoms off Amazon and the ones that look best on me are the ones that are straight-up about being "cheeky". I think for many women these will be ~accidentally cheeky~ and that's not often the most flattering option (but go on girl, it's yo world). All that said, I'm definitely going to wear these to swim and body surf in the ocean this summer. Big waves will not budge these bottoms and that's worth a lot. Also, the fabric is very nice-- opaque, stretchy, soft, solid stitching, and fully lined everywhere. In the pics I'm wearing these bottoms as a set with the "Smart & Sexy Women's Swim Secret Mega Push-up Halter Bikini Top" which I love and recommend enthusiastically.

I am torn in my review of this bathing suit between 3 and 4 stars, 3.5 stars is more appropriate. I think this tankini will fit those with a fuller figure the best. I am 5’5”, 118 lbs, 34B wearing a small. It fits me very loose so I need more neck strap support. The neck strap is very stretchy and hard to tie tight. Here are my pros and cons.Pro’s: I really love love the vivid pink color (read my cons on the color) and the front look of the tankini. The front design is visually very attractive and hides imperfections:) The front of the whole bathing suit is lined. The twisted boob area is flattering. The bra is sewn in and the cups are not flimsy inserts you can remove. The fabric is light, thin and soft.Con’s: The straps that tie around the neck are too stretchy and loosens quickly. Adjustable shoulder to the back straps would have made the top more secure. The tag mentions this garments distinctive looked is derived from a special fluorescent die, which is prone to fading. It will be a shame if this top fades drastically :( I only suggest quickly handwashing, adding vinegar to the water (do not let it sit and soak) and not wearing any light colored clothing over this tankini, especially if the tankini is damp or wet, it could bleed onto other clothing!!!! It’s advised not to hang this garment until it’s dry?? Be aware this is a high maintenance very pretty swim top. I will update on the fading issue with continued use.UPDATE: hand washed in cold water with a mild soap and added a little vinegar to the tib of water. Soaked a few minutes then rinsed. There was minimal to no color bleeding or fading. Line dried.

I generally have a really hard time with buying bikini sets because I'm a different size on the top and on the bottom. I usually need a small in bottoms and an XS in tops. Negative first--the reason I took off a star is because the dye leeches under certain circumstances. I've used and washed these swimmers about two times with no issue. The first time I wore them from the packaging, I used them in the pool, rinsed with tap water and they were fine. First time washed in washing machine they were fine too, no dye leeching into anything else. The latest time I washed them, pink dye ended up on a towel I washed with them and I thought "my bad, shouldn't have done that" so I'm not upset about that--just wash separately. But after my last swim in the pool, all I did was rinse them out with tap water in the shower and leave them to dry, when I came back to puddles of pink water underneath them. So it could have been something in the last wash that changed the dye integrity. This doesn't phase me too much as I'll just wash them separately and I haven't noticed any dye leaking in the pools I've used. Positives second-- I absolutely LOVE the fit! I am a small size (4ft 11in, 100lb) and they cover all the right places. Full coverage of my bigger bum, higher waist that comes just over my belly button and generally comfortable to be in all day. The tank top is adjustable and the foam pads can be moved where you want, then actually STAY there. I really love the pink and yellow color and I'd recommend these to anyone looking for a good, general swimsuit!

If you are being skeptical or on the fence about these thong bottoms…. buy these thong bottoms!!We do a lot of floating on the river, normally in kayaks, but I recently switched to a paddle board. For YEARS I’ve worn short shorts as swimsuit bottoms (with a cover up) cause I’ve always been soooo self conscious to wear anything else. Now using a paddle board, and telling myself I need to stop worrying about what others think (which is so hard for me to do!) talked me into trying thongs (and without a cover up!) since I can now lay flat while floating and work on my tan! I’m sooo glad I decided to try these, in fact I’m so happy with them, I ordered a second color, and planning to get more!Now for the size. I tried a medium, cause I don’t see myself as being that small (5’4”/150lbs) but I felt the material bunched a little too much in the front no matter how tight or loose I tied the sides. The smalls lay flatter and fit so much better, even tho there’s a little less material/coverage. (FYI, the tag inside the medium actually says M/L and the tag inside the small says XS/S, so keep that in mind when ordering). Also, if you want a pair that fits higher above the hips, these may not be for you. On me, they tend to gap a lot in the back, at the top, if they are pulled up too high over my hips. It seems they’re made to be worn more straight across your body, pretty much in a triangle. Hope this helps!

It is almost impossible to find a long-skirted swimsuit on Amazon. I bought another one a week ago, and it was just so high! This one looked just a little bit longer, so I tried it. Even tho they looked almost the same, this one hides my trouble spots (thighs) very well! It may not look very long, but on a 5' 5 person, it is quite nice! Also the chest part works very good for smaller chests. When I lean forward, you can't see 'them.' And of course the built in padding helps. So I would say this swimsuit could accommodate a size AA - C nicely, maybe even bigger. Love that the straps are adjustable. The color was true to what I saw online. (Which I cannot say the same for Modlily. I was disappointed with the color I got vs. what I saw.) My only disappointment was that it did not help me squish in my tummy. I picked a busy design, so it's not as bad as if I had a solid color, but I wish it had given me at least a little help there with some elastic. So if you hate anything constricting your tummy, you will find this suit delightful. The boy boxer bottoms are very good. Another pair I bought on Amazon had boy boxers, and they were too tight on my thighs. These feel just right. I got a large (correct size for me), and I'm 5'5 and 136 lb. (And fyi, I am like many other reviewers on here, who don't really love to post pics of themselves online for the world to see, especially in a swimsuit, but I am doing it to help the other ladies make their decisions. Also, to thank those of you who also post pics of yourselves to help us make our decisions. This is my payback/pay-it-forward. :) ) By the way, I like it so much, I'm going to buy another right now. Thanks for reading!

The suit fits perfectly like other reviews have mentioned. I was very surprised. There’s ruching on the lower front part, but it doesn’t add thickness to your waist and abdomen. The green color is prettier than the photo, sexy but modest. The back seat is not up too high and embarrassing (I’m 60) or down too low and matronly. The top covers side boob and stops at a spot on the back that, for me, doesn’t cause “flab spill-over”. The bottom sides come up to the perfect level for me. I’m petite, 5’ tall, 115# and usually there’s way to much length in a one piece for me, but this fits just right. I think you’ll have to check how it fits 5’6”+ ladies considering that I don’t see a lot of extra length here. Although I have another one piece green suit, I’ll keep this. It’s more of a jewel color, slightly blue-green, that I imagine would compliment many complexions. I’m Caucasian with a medium skin tone, silver-blonde hair, green eyes. My only complaint is that it arrived smelling faintly of smoke. It was clean with the crotch plastic in place, but this is a reminder that we are apparently passing these swimsuits around, so wash them thoroughly before wearing and try them on at home with undergarments as you would at the store. The smell went away after laundering. I used W**lite in the washer and even dried it in the dryer since it obviously had been worn. The seams stood up and the fit didn’t change. It isn’t made out of the most expensive fabric, so I don’t know how nice it’ll keep or for how long, but it’s a fraction of the price I’ve paid for suits that fit worse. I’d highly recommend with the aforementioned caveat that it had been tried on probably a couple time by others

I have never bought clothing online before so I ordered 7 swimsuits from Amazon. I am keeping this one and sending the others back (due to fit). I love this one. I am 5'6" and 110 pounds. I don't like to show a lot of skin and I usually wear a one piece with a pair of board shorts or other type of women's swim shorts over that. I decided to try a skirted swimsuit, as I have not bought a new suit for over 11 years. I am very small chested, so I do not even fill out the top part, however, it is formed and padded a lot (one part of the padded part can be removed). In some tops like that you can tell that I am super small and not filling it out. In this one you cannot tell. I love how the straps are thick in width and the cross in the back. It is a very well made suit and I love the feel of the material. This suit is pink and not red. I don't really like red, and I am NOT a pink person but because I feel good when I wear this suit I am willing to wear pink. I am posting 2 photos. These second one shows the shorts and the white part that connects the suit to the shorts.
