I ordered this suit in a size small navy. It fits as expected. The blue is a bit dull but I don't dislike it. I don't usually wear one piece swimsuits because they have no support. This was pretty cute so I thought I'd give it a try. It is a bit more supportive than most one piece, but still not much support. I'm 5'1", 110#, 32DDD.The fabric of this suit is a bit different. It is a somewhat thin ribbed fabric that is fully lined. I like the ribbing and the fabric is comfortable. The ruffle details are really cute. I like the design a lot.There are removable pads in the cups, but oddly enough they are round for some reason rather than triangles. You can remove and/or replace them through a buttonhole in the side of the cup.I like that the back is open for tanning. I'm not a big fan of suits that come the whole way up my back. this has a cute strap across the back (like a two piece would). It is not adjustable but it might help with support if it were.The tag says it is UPF 50 (allows 2% UV transmission). I didn't notice that on the website.The shoulder straps are adjustable and as a short girl I need to pull them almost to their tightness limit.I have one big negative quality flaw. If this hadn't happened I would have rated high for quality. I don't see any defects, imperfections, or cheapness. However, when I tried it on the first time (haven't had it on in the water yet) as I removed the shoulder straps one of them came undone at the seam. Nothing is torn. No hanging threads. It just appears as though this strap didn't get sewn. It does look like it will be easy for me to take to my sewing machine, which also means I could shorten the straps if I wanted to.But even with that flaw I quite like this suit.

As a 45 y.o. mom, I needed something a bit less "cheeky" and these bottoms delivered a great fit, stayed put even at the beach in some fierce waves. Overall great quality for a great price! Had to post a review since I went into something of a rabbit hole perusing literally hundreds to inform my choice - thanks to all the brave ladies for putting your photos & size info on Amazon. Joining you here! I ordered both a SMALL and a MEDIUM to try, I'm 5'6" 135 pounds. When I did my measurements per the size chart, Medium seemed to be the choice (I measured my hips at 32", waist maybe 26/27") but I threw a Small in my cart too since that's what I usually wear. Ended up returning the Medium, Small it is! (Small pictured here.) All seams lay flat and cute side detailing better quality than expected for the price! These even stayed on at the beach! Super surprised since this is the first time in years I've gotten bottoms for less than $40-60 (I usually buy from Athleta, Prana or similar), I'm super active in the water & can't mess around with low quality. Wanted red, white or blue for a 4th of July party & knew these would be delivered more quickly than the catalogs. Glad I took a chance on these! Deducted one star for "flattering" only because of the slight U-shape they make below my belly, you can see that in the straight-on photo. Maybe that's how they're supposed to be styled but I find myself trying to pull up & over so the top lays straight across.

Finally a bikini bottom that does not expose half or more of my butt. I do have biniki bottoms that are skimpy but I don't always have a nice butt day if that makes sense. That's where this nice bikini bottom comes in. This bottom is not high waisted which is appreciated. I like many high waisted bottoms like shorts, leggings, but I'm not convinced high waisted bikini bottoms look good on me. This full coverage bottom is comfortably snug. The little ruch and bows on the sides add to the fun and make this bottom not too plain. The inside of this bottom is not white which I'm really glad. I find white insides a bit awkward and easily stained. This bottom will also be my go to when I go to the pool or beach to be a bit more active. There are times when I play sporty activities in the water and I want something on my butt that will stay on, that won't slide left and right, that I don't have to worry about often. Overall, a good and reliable biniki bottom. I'm 5"3, 28 waist, 36 hips, 122 lbs, and got a small.

This is nice and supportive thanks to the wide chest band and thick straps that are more substantial than standard bras. The straps adjust for a better fit which is good because the cups were really wonky on me. I don't fill out a size small apparently so even with the pads in, the chest area was loose and weird. Tightening the straps helped with this although I find the fabric covering the cups to still be a bit excessive on me so that I end up with wrinkles and creases and the cups don't lay smooth. If you are bigger, this probably won't be a problem. I am a 34b but may be closer to an A cup now and this is too big in the cups for me. This is ok as far as comfort goes but it's not the most comfortable bra I own. It feels bulky around the armpits and chest band and I am aware of its presence unlike some really good bras that just blend with your body seamlessly. Quality is good.

I was really excited for this swimsuit to come in, I fell in love with the design and the pictures on the model made it look like my dream swimsuit, however, I was quite disappointed with the fit when I tried it on. The other reviews were absolutely correct about it feeling like you have to tug and pull on this swimsuit to get it to be in the correct places. The swimsuit is just so clingy and has absolutely no tummy control and, if anything, it reveals your belly even more. The built in bra was also disappointing. It was padded, but not very much and the way the built in bra part sat on the chest made me look super flat chested and it just did not look flattering, so I actually wore another padded swimsuit top I had underneath it for support. My next big complaint was the area where it transitioned into a skirt. It felt like it was placed too low, which just made it look and feel awkward. I think the placement of that area is what makes it not fit like it should and what makes it look unflattering. I ended up feeling so insecure that I just threw some beach pants over it (last pic). I ended up keeping the swimsuit (mostly because I'm too lazy to return it 🤪) and I'm hoping that maybe I will feel differently about it next time I wear it. I am 5'3 and about 127 lbs for reference and I ordered a small.

Wow, I am so surprised by how much I love this swimsuit. I'm 5'4", 130lbs, small bust, and the size small fit snugly but comfortably. I normally don't like one-piece suits because I have scoliosis, and one-pieces over-accentuate my misaligned hips. However, the structure and style of this suit mask it while also really flattering my figure. It even seems to slim down my waist and postpartum tummy. I love the straight neckline, and the built-in bra provides good support without mashing down my boobs. The thicker straps and the strings in the back are other nice details that add to this swimsuit's femininity and style. And the material is thick and feels nice, not cheap. And the turquoise color is, ugh, to die for!! It's very true to the color you see in the listing photos and is so attractive on my pale skin! I haven't worn it in water yet, but overall this suit has impressed me, and I can't wait to try it out this summer.

However, the structure and style of this suit mask it while also really flattering my figure. It even seems to slim down my waist and postpartum tummy. I love the straight neckline, and the built-in bra provides good support without mashing down my boobs. The thicker straps and the strings in the back are other nice details that add to this swimsuit's femininity and style. And the material is thick and feels nice, not cheap. And the turquoise color is, ugh, to die for!! It's very true to the color you see in the listing photos and is so attractive on my pale skin! I haven't worn it in water yet, but overall this suit has impressed me, and I can't wait to try it out this summer.

I purchased this bathing suit for my upcoming 50th Birthday Girls getaway trip to Hawaii. I wanted a suit that was fun and flattering. Not an easy ask, but this suit delivered. I’m 5’4”, a size 4 in most clothes, long waisted, 32DD and generally “fit”, but had a c-section 15 years ago that my stomach never recovered from. As a result, bathing suit shopping can be very frustrating since so much of it is about the tummy. This suit conceals my tummy issues (without making my skin crunch) while simultaneously giving a body lengthening affect. Also, the hip straps lengthen the look of legs by revealing skin all the way up. I am slightly long waisted, so II had to tug a bit to get the suit top closer to my neck, but it ultimately worked. Finally, the cut of the back of the suit has a modem looking cheeky effect without actually being cheeky. I hope you find this review . Good luck suit shopping and please remember to be extra kind to yourself when trying on suits.

i’m 5’4” and weigh 105lbs - bought size small. as a mom to three young kiddos, i’ve got some wrinkled skin below my belly button (and a small hernia which you can see in the pictures) from getting huge and pregnant, to now being slender and fit, so i wanted to find a bikini that wasn’t up to my armpits, but one that covered up my mommy tummmy. the top is darling, great fit. i’m 32DD and even in the size small it fits me very well. the bottoms, not so much. i had a loose extra inch on both sizes.. not to mention the crotch was saggy by about two inches. luckily i knew how to sew, so i made some quick alterations. overall: happy with the fit (now) and the color & style. very unique and darling. if i didn’t know how to sew i would have had to send it back, hence the 4 star rating.

My dog chewed the crotch out of this summers bathing suit so I needed one before the weekend and didn’t have time to go shopping so I searched amazon since I knew I could get one in at least a day. I chose this bathing suit because I’m a mom of two and I like one piece suits typically because I’ve never tried on a high wasted bikini bottom that I liked. One pieces can be a pain though especially when you have to go to the bathroom so I figured I’ll try this based on the positive reviews. Worst case scenario I could return it. And I really thought there was a good chance I would. Especially after running to kohl’s on my lunch break to try on 30 swim pieces and I didn’t like any of them. So...when I got home and tried this suit on I was pleasantly surprised. First of all I LOVE the bottoms and will use them with the top it came with but it will also look good with my other tops. The bottom is flattering and not tight or loose (well the butt is slightly loose but I’m ok with it because I mean it when I say slightly. Better than tight and have to deal with muffin top or whatever). I like the ruching of the fabric. The bottom is super cute. The top isn’t my typical style but together this suit is super cute. I really like it. The top seems to have a lot of room but fits at the same time. If I could have an extra small in the top I think I would like it more but the small is totally wearable and I’m looking forward to wearing this out. It’s cute and comfortable and I’m pleasantly surprised. For a size reference I’m 5’5 and 110 pounds.

But I won't be ordering a replacement due to the color being off.

The first swim dress I received had a major quality control issue. A portion of the skirt looked as if it had been hastily cut with a pair of scissors. Thankfully, the seller sent a replacement right away that did not have any fabric missing.I am 5’4” and slightly pear shaped. My measurements fall within a size small for every category of the size chart. Something that doesn’t always happen for me. That said, I could hear threads snapping as I barely squeezed my 37.5” hips into each swim dress and yet no visible change was noticeable. The dress looks good on and I am glad I went with print instead of a solid given the sheerness of the skirt. I have a long torso and felt like I’d maxed out the length of the torso, so this may not be enough length for a truly tall person. The straps are adjustable and while i could have lengthened the straps more, this would have resulted in the chest not being covered at the correct height. The raw hem of the skirt does make me a bit nervous about longevity, but since I plan to wear this more for occasionally building sand castles on the beach, then regular swimming in a pool, it should be able to get me through a summer or two.Overall: the look is good, the seller is responsive if there is an issue, and it’s probably a better fit if your hips are smaller than what is listed and your torso is on the somewhat short to medium end of the spectrum.

I was really excited about this suit but sadly, it just doesn't work for me. It seems relatively well made given the price, but the color is nowhere close to what I'd call Rose Red. I'd describe this as a flashy, hot pink. The color is really delightful and just screams tropical beach vacation.When I tried on the suit, my first reaction was that the top seems to be cut for a halter style strap, but sewn with standard straps. I tried to demonstrate why I thought this with the included pictures. When I have the strap laying straight up over my shoulder, there's a gap under the armpit that's quite obvious. If I tug the strap close to my neck, there's no gap. I inspected the suit to see if I could change the straps, but they're sewn in place. After reading a few other reviews, I am wondering if this suit isn't meant for someone with a larger chest. I wear a 32DD bra and ordered a small because the band isn't adjustable and felt a medium would be too loose. Unfortunately, I suspect that this suit is really only workable for someone 32C or smaller.The bottoms fit well enough. Since I have a relatively short torso, the bottoms completely cover my belly button. The butt has nice coverage.So this suit will not work for me, but if you're a smaller bra size, give it a shot! I'm really disappointed because I love the color.

*Using my significant other’s account to write this review* Definitely recommend this suit for the summer!! I was skeptical as I have never ordered a bathing suit from Amazon before and have trouble with 2 piece sets that are one size but pleasantly surprised. Fits perfectly! My lower half is smaller than my upper half (small butt and larger chest) so usually have to size up in swim tops but if I had sized up, both pieces would have been too big. The top fits just right especially because there’s no way to adjust it, and I don’t feel too squished and coverage is good. The bottoms right just right as well, don’t dig into my sides and very flattering. I am not a fan of high waisted suits on me but these bottoms hit just right, below the belly button. They are cheeky in the back so you have to be comfortable with showing some skin. Very comfortable as well. I went ahead and ordered a second suit in the snakeskin print because I loved it so much! I do have to pull the top up ever so often but having a larger chest, that’s the norm for strapless tops for me. I probably wouldn’t participate in water sports in this suit but perfect for laying on the beach and tanning. For reference, I’m approximately 5’3, 123-125lbs, 32C. Measurements are 32” bust, 26” waist, 34” hips. I’m typically a size small and between a size 2-4, depending on the brand.

So I've bought this in nearly every color now, and therefore feel obligated to leave a review. The first one I bought (in a medium) I actually thought to return due to the length (but ended up giving it to my taller sister). I've since bought them all in size small & I could seriously live in these. I originally bought the first one to use as a beach cover up but when my back got badly sunburned it was the only comfortable thing to wear & I started sleeping in it (whereupon I ordered two more colors). Sometimes I'll put a bra on & wear it as a dress. I love them so much I was telling people I barely knew about them because everyone should have one, or at least something as comfortable and sexy and easy as I find these. I've had to get rid of sleep shirts to make room for them all in my pajama drawer, totally worth it. Now that you know how very much I love these I'll explain why. First, I'm just at 5 ft and about 105-110 lbs. The small hits at mid thigh in the front (just a coupe inches above my knee) and the back hits right behind my knees. The sides sweep up though which gives it some small sex appeal with the peek of the thigh. The pockets are situated perfectly to hide any nip showing, which makes them so perfect for around the house as you don't want the neighbors popping by and getting an eyeful. I think the sleeves are supposed to be 3/4 but they hit right at my wrist so it's basically just a very loose long sleeve. The fabric is sheer, the white more then any other, but with skin colored underwear it can still be worn. I still reserve the white for pjs and swimsuit cover-up. The dark blue, medium blue and nude ones can all be worn as dresses, I wear dark underwear or nude colored depending on which color though. I just ordered the dark red, but I imagine it'll be similar as far as opacity. I wear these around the house for the most part and my husband said it was hard to keep his hands off me when I wear them. So of course I wear them as much as possible. Upon reading other reviews I was hesitant to wash them in anything but cold water. However, since the first was a medium I washed it in hot, hoping to shrink it some, and it didn't shrink. I've since washed all of them (except red since I haven't gotten it yet) on warm & dried them as normal in the dryer, and they've all maintained their color, size, shape, and soft breathable texture. If the red fades upon washing/drying I'll update my review, but it won't change my ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ rating regardless. A couple have come with a few loose threads here and there but it's not a big deal to me. The only thing I would change about them (except to add even more colors) is the pockets. They are actually pockets but they're not functional because the button is sewn down to it, with no button hole. I could fix this with my sewing machine but it isn't worth the hassle to me. Maybe next time I drag the giant thing out but until then I'll live with them as merely aesthetic. All in all, these are well worth having... in your pajama drawer, your beach bag or hanging in your closet. If you're on the thinner side but tall, and don't want anything approaching mini-dress, get a medium, as they're not much bigger in fit but are a bit longer. If you're curvier but still on the short side, and don't want it hitting your knees (in the front, or below the knee in the back) go for a small still as even the small has a very loose flowy fit. However, if you have a large chest (I'm a 30 D/32 C) so I mean larger than, you might be more comfortable in a medium as the chest could get snug if you don't want the buttons undone too far (which would probably look hot with such cleavage, but still...) Anyway, hope that helps someone avoid having to buy two sizes as I had, but even if not, totally worth the hassle in the long run cause these really are all that and a bag of chips.

Fairly true to size but runs slightly small. I’m an XS/S bottom 36-37” hip and an athletic 32C bust and the S fits perfectly. Note that not all of the back straps adjust, only the bottom most strap connecting one side to the other is adjustable. The x-pattern on the straps are fixed, but where they intersect slides through the other straps, so there is a little adjusting that can happen. However, this fits like a dream! The bottoms NEVER moved while boogie boarding for an hour and a half in the Florida surf, and I never once had to dig them out of my crack. These are low rise and cheeky, which is exactly what I wanted. The S top shows off enough but also doesn’t budge, so I don’t feel like I’m ever going to stand up out of the water showing off a nipple. This is my second summer with this suit and it still looks brand new. Very well made. The bottoms are a hot-pink/bright cherry but not neon. It’s a really pretty color and the colors in the top match it perfectly. I love that every seam is an interior rolled elastic edge so there’s no pinching. Buying a second one before it’s no longer available!

This 2 piece high-waisted swimsuit is a beautiful deep shade of red. At first glance it looks like a perfect fit but if you look at several pictures I’ve circled where it gaps a little and gathers funny along the outside edge of the top. I used their size chart and chose my usual S based on the bottoms so they would fit. For reference I’m 5’3”, 124 lbs, 34D, 37-27-37. I’m a little busty so usually when I get a S it’s a little snug or small in the cup. This fits my band pretty good, but the cups are slightly big and gap a little on the sides which is where I’m fullest. I’d say this suit will work best if you’re quite busty. If this top was any bigger I wouldn’t be comfy in it and if I had ordered the M based on my regular bra size the whole suit would be really big on me. There’s a lot to like about this suit, here’s my thoughts Pros: love the pullover style, so comfy without a back clasp, adjustable shoulder straps are a must for me, well made with quality fabric, there are plastic stays to support the seam where cups & band meet, love the high waist, the mesh trim is nice and there’s just enough ruching in the bottoms to help hide little sins like too much ice cream! Cons: sizing is way off on top, wish the bottom coverage was a little bit wider, ubiquitous triangle loose pads in top please y’all manufacturers and designers just sew them in or leave them out completely. I’ll probably remove these and if it’s too nippley I’ll wear silicone petals. I think that the pros far outweigh the cons and at less than $20 currently I highly recommend this suit especially for the ladies with larger chests!

Cons: sizing is way off on top, wish the bottom coverage was a little bit wider, ubiquitous triangle loose pads in top please y’all manufacturers and designers just sew them in or leave them out completely. I’ll probably remove these and if it’s too nippley I’ll wear silicone petals. I think that the pros far outweigh the cons and at less than $20 currently I highly recommend this suit especially for the ladies with larger chests!

I got an XL. I'm 5'6", about 167lbs, dress size 8, measurements are 42" bust, 29.5" waist, 43" hips/butt, and a cup size 34H. Im DEFINITELY curvy and struggle immensely with bikinis because of my natural bust. I took a chance on this bikini and I was really impressed! The top was more than big enough to hold and cover my chest. Although, as predicted, strapless it struggled to support my 20lbs breasts. I could get away with it, even jump without them flying out. But it just wasnt the look I wanted. The straps were ok in their normal position, definitely held me better. I got creative and criss crossed the straps in the back. Which made it tighter, slightly more supportive, and added some cute flair. Also tried criss crossing over the front and that was also very cute but I wanted to avoid weird tan lines on my chest. The adjustability of this bikini is awesome! Of course after moving around my breasts would slide down and I'd have to adjust periodically but that's honestly just part of have big heavy boobs so I dont really fault the bikini. I DEFINITELY took out the padding because it looked bizarre on my body. But amazingly, my nipples didnt poke through all that much. The bottom fit like a dream, although it is tastefully cheeky. The waist was a smidge too big for me but frankly I'm used to that since I have such a huge difference between my waist and hips and some pudge. For the price it's awesome. Fabric is very comfy. The color is so gorgeous! Its vibrant and bright, exactly what I wanted. For an Amazon bikini it's very nice. Supposedly you cannot machine wash it? Which is a downer but eh there are worse things. Overall, if you're curvy, busty, big booty, have no fear of this bikini. Though dont expect great support for big busts. Almost gave it 4 stars because of the not so great support and the no machine wash thing. But those are pretty minor imo

I really liked this bikini. Likely, I ordered size XL. Normally, I wear M (6-8 bottoms and 34DD top). Because of the top size I often order two suits in different size, but this is one is great. I am definitely taking it to my upcoming vacation trip. Usually, when the bikini bottoms are too big, they have too much fabric I the front, but these are perfectly adjustable and cheeky, so XL will fit anything M-L, no likely it will fit size 12 and above. The color is a bit shiny Paris blue, it is amazing for any skin color. There are small metallic details that are subtle but add the charm. The fabric is elastic, it hugs the curves well, looks the same wet or dry. The bra and the front of the bottoms are lines with very thin white fabric, which is sewn so that it won’t peek out, the seam is a broad overlock of blue. There are removable pads. I never use those, but can say these are good, they follow the shape of the bra closely. Overall, I recommend this bikini, it is pretty, classic and well made.

I have a wide back and 38D cup, I was worried that it wouldn’t fit or be supportive but I was pleasantly surprised!

First off, the bottoms fit as expected. The top runs a little small around the ribcage and it can’t be adjusted so if you’ve got a booty that makes the rockin’ world go ‘round and a smaller upper body/ribcage like I do, then this suit is right up your alley. I got an XL for my booty to fit (5’4” 150lbs 36B) It was a little bit too big in the cups for me so I’d say if you have a FULL B cup or C-D, it would fit you better than it fits me on top. It’s a tad cheeky in the back, but cute. As far as the stitching goes, I feel that’s where it’s lacking (and the inability to adjust around the ribcage). My bottoms had 3 spots where the stitching went too far and was sticking out and kept popping out. If I trim it, it may fall apart. Overall, for twenty doll hairs, it’s not terrible but not my fav swimsuit ever.

I like this tankini. The hibiscus flower pattern is exotic, and it is cut to be tight in the bust and flare out very wide over the hips. I am a somewhat pear-shaped 42C, and the bust on the extra large was just barely large enough for me, but there were inches and inches to spare in the hips (although the swimming trunks that accompany it were a bit tight on me). The design of this tankini means it would probably work well as a maternity swimsuit, except for the fact that it would then be shorter in the front. What really surprised me is that this suit is not hemmed at the bottom—it is just cut off! I don’t know if mine was somehow unfinished or if it was intended to be made that way. It is not frayed in any way, so perhaps it will be fine, but I am tempted to take out my sewing machine and hem it! I’m giving it 4 stars rather than 5 because of the lack of a hem. Apart from that, it’s a nice swimsuit and one that will work especially well for anyone who is pear-shaped or even pregnant.

I’m 5’2”, ~170lbs, wear an 8-10 range in pants and dresses and a 34D if I put on a real bra. I’ve got a pretty good sized butt and a lower stomach to counter it. Because of this, and the fact that I simply stopped reading size charts and went with review trends from people that look anything remotely like my shape, I got an XL. The whole suit is a great material and I really like the deep green. The clasp is just the metal hook that slides into a loop on the other side of the upper band. The bottoms fit me perfectly — I love the ruching on the back and the v-shape / fold in the front. The top is just too big. I loved it so much I thought I could just throw in a stitch to tighten the lower band (and be okay opting for full coverage in place some titty peak-a-boo), but then I started to feel like if I wanted the actual top to be secure, I’d need the strap to be tighter (as pictured) and even more so if I wanted some boob action. Great suit that only lost a star because I’m convincing myself that my body is no longer the problem, it’s the *clothing* that doesn’t fit *me* right. If the top was adjustable or you could buy the pieces in two sizes, I’d be all over this. I hope this helps someone who has had a tumultuous time shopping for swimmies. I hope you find joy in whatever swimsuit you end up in, because you deserve it.
